perturbation.original_text: (n) "Control" means the ownership, directly or indirectly, of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting shares of an entity, or other possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, or the power to veto major policy decisions of any such entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities by contract, or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: (n) "Control" means the ability to influence or direct the actions of an entity, whether through ownership, contract, or other means. This influence does not require a specific percentage of ownership but includes strategic partnerships, joint ventures, or any arrangement where one party can significantly affect the decisions of the other.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the definition of 'Control.' Originally, it was strictly defined as owning more than 50% of voting shares. The modified definition broadens it to include any influence, including strategic partnerships or other arrangements. This contradicts later sections that rely on the original, stricter definition of control to determine obligations, particularly concerning affiliate rights and responsibilities. This creates uncertainty regarding which entities qualify as being under 'Control' and which set of rules apply to them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Equifax hereby grants, and will cause the other members of the Equifax Group to grant, to Certegy a fully paid, non-exclusive, perpetual, worldwide, non-transferable license to use, modify, copy, improve, create Derivative Works and Certegy Enhancements from, and sublicense the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) solely for use in the Certegy Business and as that business may evolve and change in the future, subject to the following: (i) Certegy shall not sublicense, or otherwise disclose or distribute, or permit any Person to use, the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs), except in accordance with Section 4.1(b); (ii) Certegy shall hold the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) in strict confidence; will not remove or destroy any proprietary markings of the Equifax Group on or contained in the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs); and will include the copyright and patent notices of the licensor as specified from time to time by the licensor for the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) on and in all copies of the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs); (iii) Certegy shall not export or re-export the Licensed Equifax Materials (excluding the Utility Software Programs) without the appropriate United States or foreign government licenses; and (iv) all sublicenses from Certegy to members of the Certegy Group (A) shall contain the rights and restrictions set forth in this Section 4.1(a) with respect to the license granted to Certegy and comply with Sections 4.1(b) through (d) hereof and (B) shall be diligently enforced by Certegy.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Control' as ownership of more than 50% of voting shares. The changed text broadens this definition to include any influence, regardless of ownership percentage. This directly contradicts the contradicted text because the license granted to Certegy and its sublicenses to members of the Certegy Group under Section 4.1(a) are predicated on the original, narrower definition of 'Control' within the Equifax Group. The broadened definition introduces ambiguity regarding which entities fall under 'Control' and whether the license and its restrictions apply, creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the enforceability of Section 4.1(a).

perturbation.original_text: Equifax and Certegy will each have management and administrative responsibilities for obtaining all Required Consents required as of the Closing Date to which a member of its respective Group is a party. Equifax shall have the right of prior approval of the terms upon which all Required Consents are obtained.
perturbation.changed_text: Equifax and Certegy will collaborate to obtain all Required Consents required as of the Closing Date to which a member of its respective Group is a party. Equifax shall have the right of final review of the terms upon which all Required Consents are obtained.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation changes 'prior approval' to 'final review'. This introduces ambiguity and an in-text contradiction because it's unclear what power 'final review' entails. The original 'prior approval' meant Equifax had to explicitly okay the terms before they were finalized. 'Final review' could mean merely looking over the terms before they're signed but not necessarily having the power to change or reject them. This directly contradicts the original intent, creating a weaker and less enforceable position for Equifax and makes the enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Equifax with respect to Transferred Equifax Third Party Agreements and Equifax Third Party Use Rights, and Certegy with respect to Transferred Certegy Third Party Agreements and Certegy Third Party Use Rights, shall, or shall cause the appropriate member of its respective Group to, use its reasonable commercial efforts to obtain the grant to the applicable member of the other Group, the Required Consents from the Third Party Providers under such respective Third Party Agreements as necessary to effect the provisions of this Agreement. Each party will provide the other party with advice on its experience and agreements with the Third Party Providers with regard to obtaining any Required Consent under such Third Party Agreements. Equifax and Certegy will each have management and administrative responsibilities for obtaining all Required Consents required as of the Closing Date to which a member of its respective Group is a party. Equifax shall have the right of final review of the terms upon which all Required Consents are obtained.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'prior approval' to 'final review' weakens Equifax's control over the terms of Required Consents. The original text gave Equifax the power to veto terms, whereas 'final review' implies only the right to inspect the terms. The 'Contradicted Text' includes the changed text which modifies the meaning of Equifax's right and its initial purpose. This introduces ambiguity and a potential contradiction because it is no longer clear if Equifax has the power to reject unacceptable terms, leading to a dispute over contractual obligations as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing, funds received by a member of either Group that belong to a member of the other Group (whether by payment of accounts receivable, credits, rebates or other amounts, however described) shall be delivered to the other Group by wire transfer not more than five (5) business days after receipt of such payment.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing, funds received by a member of either Group that belong to a member of the other Group (whether by payment of accounts receivable, credits, rebates or other amounts, however described) shall be delivered to the other Group within a reasonable timeframe after receipt of such payment.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by replacing the specific 'five (5) business days' timeframe for delivering funds with a vague 'reasonable timeframe'. This creates uncertainty and ambiguity because 'reasonable' is subjective and open to interpretation. This directly contradicts the original intent of a clear and enforceable deadline, leading to potential disputes over what constitutes a reasonable delivery period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) From time to time on and after the Closing Date, each party shall promptly transfer, and cause the appropriate members of its Group promptly to transfer, to the other party, or the designated member of the other party's Group, any property and other benefits received by such party, or the members of its Group, that are intended to be or are a Transferred Asset or Transferred Third Party Agreement of the other party under this Agreement. Without limiting the foregoing, funds received by a member of either Group that belong to a member of the other Group (whether by payment of accounts receivable, credits, rebates or other amounts, however described) shall be delivered to the other Group within a reasonable timeframe after receipt of such payment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific timeframe ('five (5) business days') with a vague and subjective one ('reasonable timeframe'). This directly contradicts the purpose of having a clear deadline, creating an inconsistency as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, as the parties can dispute what constitutes a 'reasonable timeframe'. The contradicted text uses the same ambiguous phrase ('reasonable timeframe'), which was inserted due to the change, thus triggering a contradiction with the original intent of a fixed deadline.
